name: bash
description:
Execute a shell command in the session output directory.

Use for: data transformation (jq, awk, sort, cut), system inspection (wc, file, du), running scripts, and operations not covered by dedicated file tools.

Prefer dedicated tools for file reading, writing, editing, searching, and pattern matching — this tool is for everything else.

CWD is the session output directory. Use absolute paths for files outside it. Output truncated at 20 KB — for large outputs, redirect to a file and inspect afterward. Returns stdout/stderr with exit code.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Supports pipes and standard Unix tools.

description.description: Brief human-readable description of what this command does. Logged for audit, not executed.

timeout.description: Execution timeout in milliseconds. Clamped to [1000, 120000]. Defaults to 30000 (30s).
